tax: Placed at order rank, Lithic Elementals is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: bulk-mineral composition with no crystal lattice or native-metal seam, animated by fault or seep pressure. A new family belongs here only if it shows a distinct rock-state body-plan with its own formation trigger and field signs.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a mass that matches its substrate — boulder, scree-sphere, mud-mound, or dust-column — and moves against the slope's logic. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — ground tremor or a wrong-way drag, swept groove, drying pan, or shed film left on the floor — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Lithic Elementals occupies the load-bearing terrain itself: benches, chutes, clay galleries, and dust shafts. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where a fault flexes, a seep saturates, or a dry bed takes a static charge and recur with the next tremor, refill, or recharge of the same substrate, sustaining themselves on tectonic pressure, gravity, osmotic swelling, or static earth-charge.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: members lie inert as terrain and rouse only to disturbance of their own substrate, answering as rockfall, mudslide, or duststorm. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: a contact-bound stone body, immunity to crushing, and a specific medium-failure (immersion, drought, draught, or flat ground). These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each family and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: reshaping and absorbing surrounding ground, mass or momentum attacks, and concealment as terrain. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Lithic Elementals matters because most fatal 'natural' rockfalls, mudslides, and dust events on the worked tiers are in fact roused Lithic elementals. It lets survey reports sort terrain hazards by rock-state, so a crew knows whether to fear mass, momentum, smother, or abrasion without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Lithic Elementals is the order-rank grouping of elementals whose bodies are bulk rock, rubble, clay, silt, or shaped-stone humanoid form within the True Elementals. Its members share a body of undifferentiated stone-stuff held by earth-pressure and a complete dependence on contact with the ground, and divide below into family groups by the state of the rock — solid boulder, loose rubble, wet clay, dry dust, or shaped bipedal form (Terrae-Kin). It is the most familiar earth order to Throat crews, because most 'rockfalls', 'mud-slumps', and 'stones that walk like people' fall here.
